patient.diagnosis: Post-total hip replacement, COPD, Type 2 Diabetes
patient.procedure: Total right hip replacement (total hip arthroplasty)

medications.name: Oxycodone
medications.frequency: every 4 to 6 hours as needed for pain
medications.purpose: manages moderate to severe pain after your hip surgery
medications.special_instructions: do not drive or operate machinery while taking this; take with food to avoid nausea

medications.name: Celecoxib
medications.purpose: reduces inflammation and helps with pain as the oxycodone is tapered off
medications.special_instructions: take with a full glass of water and food

medications.name: Warfarin
medications.frequency: once daily at the same time each evening
medications.purpose: prevents dangerous blood clots in your leg after hip surgery
medications.special_instructions: you will need an INR blood test on April 1st to check your Warfarin levels; avoid aspirin and ibuprofen

medications.name: Salbutamol Inhaler
medications.frequency: as needed when you feel short of breath or wheezy
medications.purpose: opens up your airways quickly to relieve COPD symptoms
medications.special_instructions: keep it with you at all times; shake before each use

medications.name: Metformin
medications.frequency: twice daily with breakfast and dinner
medications.purpose: controls your blood sugar levels for diabetes
medications.special_instructions: take with food; your blood sugar may fluctuate more during recovery — monitor closely

activity_restrictions: use your walker at all times for the first 4 weeks — no weight-bearing without it
do not bend your hip more than 90 degrees — avoid low chairs and reaching for your feet
no driving until cleared by Dr. Nguyen (typically 6 weeks)
do your daily physical therapy exercises as shown by your therapist
do not cross your legs or ankles when sitting or lying down
wound_care: keep the hip incision clean and dry
change the wound dressing every 2 days or when wet or soiled
gently pat dry after showering — do not scrub the incision
steri-strips or staples will be removed at your follow-up appointment
watch for signs of infection: increasing redness, warmth, or discharge from the wound

follow_ups.purpose: post-surgery wound check, staple removal, and mobility assessment
INR blood test to check Warfarin level for clot prevention

warning_signs: calf pain, swelling, or redness in the leg — possible blood clot, go to ER immediately
sudden shortness of breath or chest pain — call 911 immediately
increasing pain, redness, warmth, or discharge from the surgical wound
sudden severe hip pain with leg appearing shorter or turned — possible dislocation, call 911
blood sugar below 70 mg/dL (shakiness, sweating, confusion) or above 300 mg/dL
